jokes.text: Louie's joke about the man who needs a dentist because with his wife 'it's like pulling teeth'
jokes.setup: Driver asks to see dentist, Louie starts story about man with sexual problems going to dentist
jokes.punchline: Punchline reveals the double meaning of 'pulling teeth' - both dental work and difficult sexual relations
jokes.explanation: Classic setup-punchline structure with wordplay. Well-executed but fairly predictable joke type. Good character integration as Louie would tell this kind of crude story.

jokes.text: After the long joke, Latka asks 'So that means it's all right to go to the dentist?' and Louie says 'No, no, no way'
jokes.setup: Latka assumes the joke was somehow relevant to his request
jokes.punchline: Louie reveals the joke was completely irrelevant - just his way of wasting time
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and character work. Shows Louie's cruel nature and Latka's innocent confusion. Very economical payoff.

jokes.text: Latka panics and asks 'Where is the yakabye?' thinking it's an emergency
jokes.setup: Latka overhears Tony's story about the emergency call
jokes.punchline: Latka genuinely thinks 'yakabye' is a real emergency and starts looking for it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work from Latka - his foreign confusion creates perfect escalation of the absurd premise. High character integration.

jokes.text: Latka tells arriving character 'too bad... You miss Yakabye drill'
jokes.setup: Someone arrives after the yakabye confusion
jokes.punchline: Latka now treats it as if yakabye drills are a regular, serious procedure
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Latka has fully integrated the nonsense into his worldview. Strong character consistency.

jokes.text: About Tony's date who wanted to be a boxer: 'I never want to see her again especially in the ring... I wouldn't have a chance'
jokes.setup: Tony says he never wants to see his date again
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's because she could beat him up in boxing, not just general dislike
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection that subverts expectation. Good character work showing Tony's boxing pride and masculinity concerns.

jokes.text: Latka about watching football at Alex's apartment: 'It's better than watching it on television'
jokes.setup: Alex invites Latka to watch the game at his apartment
jokes.punchline: Latka thinks watching TV at someone's house is different than watching TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect Latka logic - innocent confusion delivered with complete sincerity. Excellent economy and character integration.

jokes.text: About New Hampshire boxing: 'Do they have a lot of middleweights in New Hampshire?' 'Three. The guy I'm fighting just took the title away from his father.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of Tony fighting the New Hampshire state champion
jokes.punchline: There are only three middleweights in the entire state, making it a pathetic 'dynasty'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of absurdity. The reveal that there are only three fighters total makes the 'dynasty' concept ridiculous. Great structure and economy.

jokes.text: Alex's comment: 'You're not just fighting a champ... You're fighting a whole dynasty.'
jokes.setup: Learning the New Hampshire champion beat his father for the title
jokes.punchline: Alex sarcastically calls a three-person boxing scene a 'dynasty'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurdity with Alex's dry delivery. Works as follow-up to the three fighters joke.

jokes.text: Tony about his fish: 'I bought them with the purse from my first big win... $3.00'
jokes.setup: Tony explains he bought his fish with prize money from his first big win
jokes.punchline: His 'big win' only paid three dollars
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with excellent character work. Shows Tony's humble boxing career while building emotional attachment to the fish. Great economy.

jokes.text: Latka's reaction to his toothache and getting permission to go to dentist, thanking Louie and saying people say bad things about him but...
jokes.setup: Latka gets permission for dentist after much delay
jokes.punchline: He thanks Louie and says people call him 'cheap and mean and cruel and nasty' as if defending him
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Latka's innocent attempt to defend Louie while actually listing his worst qualities.

jokes.text: About Bobby's sparse kitchen: 'There's only a loaf of old bread in here. We could have beer and toast.'
jokes.setup: Looking for food in Bobby's refrigerator
jokes.punchline: The pathetic suggestion of 'beer and toast' as a meal
jokes.explanation: Modest observational humor about Bobby's poor living situation. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Latka's response: 'No, thank you. I'm trying to watch my babas.'
jokes.setup: Offered beer and toast
jokes.punchline: Latka says he's watching his 'babas' (foreign word) instead of 'carbs'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific wordplay. Perfect for Latka's foreign background and creates a memorable made-up word.

jokes.text: Bobby about Dominique: 'You know, she turned down Warren Beatty. Imagine that. Me and Warren being turned down by the same girl.'
jokes.setup: Bobby brags about his date with Dominique
jokes.punchline: He puts himself on the same level as Warren Beatty, when she chose him
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Bobby's inflated self-regard. Nice structure with the delayed realization of the absurd comparison.

jokes.text: Latka about football teams: 'Well, you see, because this team here is called the Giants, but they are not bigger than the other team.'
jokes.setup: Latka explains why football is confusing
jokes.punchline: He takes team names literally - Giants should be bigger
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational comedy through Latka's innocent foreign perspective. Perfect character integration and fresh take on team names.

jokes.text: Latka continues: 'And also, this team is called the Redskins, but they are almost all black.'
jokes.setup: Continuation of Latka's literal interpretation of team names
jokes.punchline: Points out racial disconnect between team name and players
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that adds social commentary through innocent observation. Latka's foreignness allows him to point out absurdities Americans ignore.

jokes.text: Phone conversation confusion about how many people are rooting for Tony, escalating from 'three of us' to 'five of us'
jokes.setup: Trying to tell Tony how many friends are supporting him
jokes.punchline: The number keeps changing randomly during the conversation
jokes.explanation: Minor absurdist moment that shows the chaos of group conversations. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Latka about instant replay: 'You know, in my country, they don't have instant replay. They just make them do it over again.'
jokes.setup: Watching football replay on TV
jokes.punchline: Latka's country makes players physically repeat plays instead of showing replays
jokes.explanation: Exceptional joke that perfectly captures Latka's character while creating brilliant absurdist logic. Highly quotable and original. Near-perfect economy.

jokes.text: Visual gag: The fish floating on their backs, clearly dead
jokes.setup: Bobby was supposed to feed Tony's beloved fish
jokes.punchline: Cut to the fish floating dead in the tank
jokes.explanation: Strong visual gag that serves the story. High impact due to dramatic implications, though not particularly original as a concept.

jokes.text: Bobby's explanation for the fish deaths: 'Well, I guess it was just their time, huh?' and 'Maybe it was one of those murder-suicide things.'
jokes.setup: Trying to avoid responsibility for killing the fish
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd explanations including fish murder-suicide
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy that perfectly captures Bobby's character - avoiding responsibility with increasingly ridiculous explanations. Good escalation.

jokes.text: Louie's immediate response when Tony arrives: 'Bobby killed your fish.'
jokes.setup: Everyone's trying to figure out how to break bad news to Tony
jokes.punchline: Louie immediately blurts out the worst possible thing
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Louie - cruel, direct, and perfectly timed. Excellent economy and structure as it cuts through all the tension.

jokes.text: Tony's castle gift for his fish with 'Scranton' written on it
jokes.setup: Tony bought a gift for his fish while away
jokes.punchline: The touching gesture becomes tragic because the fish are dead
jokes.explanation: Powerful dramatic irony that builds emotional stakes. The mundane tourist gift becomes heartbreaking in context.

jokes.text: Tony's speech about his fish: 'them fish are like symbols... of, like, survival. If them little fish can survive in this world than so can I.'
jokes.setup: Tony explains his deep emotional connection to his fish
jokes.punchline: The profound statement about survival when the fish are already dead
jokes.explanation: Devastating dramatic irony with perfect character integration. The sincerity makes the tragedy more impactful. Excellent structure and earned emotion.

jokes.text: Alex's response when asked how the fish looked: 'Peaceful.'
jokes.setup: Tony asks how his fish looked yesterday
jokes.punchline: Alex describes dead fish as 'peaceful' - technically true but misleading
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan delivery that's technically honest while being completely misleading. Perfect economy and dark humor.

jokes.text: Bobby arriving with replacement fish: 'I had to go to four stores before I could find two that matched exactly.'
jokes.setup: Bobby shows up with what appear to be replacement fish
jokes.punchline: He went to great lengths to find matching fish, showing both effort and deception
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bobby's attempt to fix his mistake through elaborate deception. Shows both care and dishonesty.

jokes.text: Tony: 'Hey, where are they?' realizing his original fish are missing
jokes.setup: Tony expects to see his original fish with the new ones
jokes.punchline: The moment of realization that something is wrong
jokes.explanation: Tension-building moment as the deception unravels.

jokes.text: Bobby's confession: 'Tony, I forgot to feed them yesterday and when I came home they were...'
jokes.setup: Bobby trying to confess his mistake to Tony
jokes.punchline: The painful awkwardness of admitting he killed the fish through negligence
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe comedy as Bobby struggles with the confession. Good character work but not particularly innovative.

jokes.text: Bobby's attempt at comfort: 'I think they went in their sleep.'
jokes.setup: Bobby trying to soften the blow of the fish deaths
jokes.punchline: Uses phrase typically for natural human death for fish he killed through negligence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor in Bobby's attempt to make the situation seem peaceful. Good economy but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Bobby's plea: 'if you want to slug me... just not in the face. I got an audition.'
jokes.setup: Bobby expecting Tony to hit him for killing the fish
jokes.punchline: His main concern is protecting his face for an acting audition
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy showing Bobby's self-centered priorities even in crisis. Excellent economy and very quotable line.

jokes.text: Louie: 'He booked 90, fish killer.'
jokes.setup: Tony asking what Bobby 'booked' (got)
jokes.punchline: Louie's cruel nickname for Bobby based on the fish incident
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie cruelty. Turns tragedy into mockery with typical insensitivity.

jokes.text: Louie's immediate taxi booking comment: 'Hey, buddy, you need a quarter? I got it.'
jokes.setup: Tony storms out upset about his dead fish
jokes.punchline: Louie immediately tries to profit from Tony's emotional distress
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - cruel opportunism at exactly the wrong time. Excellent character integration and timing.

jokes.text: Elaine asking 'How many?' about Tony's rejections, Louie responding 'So far... 12.'
jokes.setup: Tony has been rejecting everyone's attempts to talk to him
jokes.punchline: Louie has been keeping an exact count of the rejections
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor showing Louie's detached interest in others' misery. Nice economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: Alex's argument to convince Tony: 'Because I think the fish would want it that way.'
jokes.setup: Alex trying to convince Tony to reconcile with Bobby
jokes.punchline: Appeals to what dead fish would want regarding human friendship
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that somehow works emotionally. Perfect economy and highly quotable. Great character moment for Alex as peacemaker.

jokes.text: Tony's philosophy: 'I mean, there are guys I don't even like that treat me better. One guy I hate treats me almost as good.'
jokes.setup: Tony questioning why he likes Bobby despite poor treatment
jokes.punchline: Even people he hates treat him better than his best friend
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that captures the absurdity of friendship loyalty. Good structure building to the punchline about someone he hates.

jokes.text: About replacement fish: 'It's a dog's life.'
jokes.setup: Looking at the doomed replacement fish
jokes.punchline: Uses 'dog's life' expression about fish who are about to die
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that works in context but isn't particularly clever or memorable. Functional humor.

jokes.text: Tony almost destroying Bobby's lucky shirt, then realizing: 'Gee, I just remembered... It's my shirt.'
jokes.setup: Tony wants to destroy Bobby's prized shirt in revenge
jokes.punchline: The shirt Bobby treasures is actually stolen from Tony
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that escalates the borrowed items theme. Perfect structure and great character revelation.

jokes.text: Tony's rambling philosophy: 'A guy can do what he can do, and he can't do what he can't do, so if a guy doesn't do what he can't do, what are you going to do?'
jokes.setup: Tony trying to explain why he couldn't destroy the shirt
jokes.punchline: Completely circular, meaningless philosophical statement delivered earnestly
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy showing Tony's attempt at wisdom resulting in complete nonsense. Highly quotable circular logic.

jokes.text: Bobby correcting fish names: 'George and Wanda!' 'Wanda! The least you could do is get their names straight! Wanda!'
jokes.setup: Tony keeps getting his dead fish's name wrong
jokes.punchline: Bobby is more upset about the name mistake than Tony is about the dead fish
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Bobby's misplaced priorities. Good character comedy with the absurdity of caring more about names than death.

jokes.text: The reconciliation moment: 'Wendy, Wanda.' 'Oh... I love you guys.'
jokes.setup: The three friends arguing about the fish situation
jokes.punchline: Simple name correction leads to emotional reconciliation
jokes.explanation: Beautiful emotional payoff with perfect character integration. Shows how small gestures can resolve big conflicts. Excellent structure.

jokes.text: Bobby's promise: 'From now on, when I say something I'm going to do it, and that's that!' followed immediately by abandoning his friends
jokes.setup: Bobby makes solemn promise to be more reliable
jokes.punchline: Immediately breaks the promise by ditching them for Dominique
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy showing Bobby's immediate return to form. Excellent structure and irony. Classic sitcom ending.

jokes.text: Bobby's decision to abandon Dominique for the guys, despite his earlier resolution about not letting people down
jokes.setup: Bobby's speech about keeping commitments and being dependable
jokes.punchline: He immediately abandons his romantic date to hang with the guys
jokes.explanation: Classic Bobby self-contradiction. His commitment to commitment is immediately broken.

jokes.text: Tony's final gesture: feeding the mounted fish
jokes.setup: Tony has his dead fish mounted as memorial
jokes.punchline: Still tries to feed the taxidermied fish
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual ending that shows Tony's character perfectly - touching and absurd simultaneously. Excellent economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Tony's presentation speech: 'Hey, can I have your attention, please, ladies and gentlemen? I have a presentation to make. For my best friend, Tony.'
jokes.setup: Bobby making public presentation of the mounted fish
jokes.punchline: Calls Tony his best friend after all the conflict
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that resolves the friendship theme. Good escalation making it public declaration.
